title: De Zerbi ready for relegation fight ‘until the last minute’ after Leeds frustrate Spurs
description: Roberto De Zerbi vowed that Tottenham will take their relegation battle to the wire after their hopes of staying up were dented by a frustrating 1-1 draw with Leeds
content:
Roberto De Zerbi vowed that Tottenham will take their relegation battle to the wire after their hopes of staying up were dented by a frustrating 1-1 draw with Leeds.

title: Mayor of California city resigns over charges of being a foreign agent of China
description: Eileen Wang, 58, mayor of Arcadia, agreed to plead guilty over the felony count brought by the justice department
content:
Eileen Wang, the mayor of a southern California city, resigned suddenly on Monday after the US Department of Justice (DoJ) announced she had been charged with acting as an illegal foreign agent of China.
Wang, 58, agreed to plead guilty to the felony... [3494 chars]

title: British Book Awards: Virginia Giuffre’s memoir wins top book prize
description: Nobody's Girl, published last year, detailed Giuffre's encounters with the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
content:
A posthumous memoir by Virginia Giuffre, one of Jeffrey Epstein’s most prominent accusers, has won the top prize at this year’s British Book Awards.
Nobody’s Girl, co-written with Amy Wallace, was named book of the year in a ceremony held in London o... [3777 chars]

title: Chris Mason: PM confronts his most difficult day as party revolts
description: A dam appears to have broken, with Labour MPs posting their public loss of confidence in the PM, our political editor writes.
content:
This morning, Sir Keir Starmer's cabinet is split on the most fundamental political question a government's top table of ministers can ever wrestle with: whether the prime minister should carry on.
Clearly, a cabinet split on this is unsustainable. E... [2618 chars]

title: Students at risk if universities go bust, say MPs
description: An Education Select Committee report finds the government needs to make urgent plans for universities facing insolvency.
content:
Students need better government protection if a university in England goes bust and cannot pay its debts, according to MPs.
In a report, the Education Select Committee said 24 institutions are now said to be at risk of insolvency within the next 12 m... [1860 chars]
